# @zakkster/lite-headless / split-panels

Headless resizable split panels. N panels separated by N-1 handles. Sizes
are percentages of the container's main axis (sum to 100). The engine
writes one CSS custom property per panel (`--lh-panel-{N}-pct`) to the
container -- never inline width/height -- so consumer CSS composes the
layout via grid or flex.

Hot-path discipline: container rect cached once on pointerdown; pointermove
does zero DOM reads. One array allocation per layout change (signal
reference-equality requirement), bounded by frame rate.

## Orientation

Both orientations are supported via the `orientation` option, matching
the convention used by sortable/carousel/slider:

- `orientation: "horizontal"` (default) -- panels arranged left-to-right
  (along the horizontal axis). Separator bars are **vertical lines**.
  Keyboard nav: ArrowLeft / ArrowRight.
- `orientation: "vertical"` -- panels arranged top-to-bottom (along the
  vertical axis). Separator bars are **horizontal lines**. Keyboard nav:
  ArrowUp / ArrowDown.

For 2D layouts (IDE-style: top section + bottom terminal, where the top
section has sidebar + editor + inspector), **nest two split-panels
instances**: the inner container IS one of the outer's panels. Two
independent layouts, two `onLayoutChange` callbacks, no shared state.
Composes for any depth without needing a 2D grid primitive.

```js
// outer: vertical (top section + terminal stacked)
const outer = createSplitPanels({ orientation: "vertical" });
outer.attachContainer(rootEl);
outer.attachPanel(topSectionEl, 0);
outer.attachPanel(terminalEl,   1, { collapsible: true });
outer.attachHandle(outerHandleEl, 0);

// inner: horizontal (sidebar / editor / inspector)
const inner = createSplitPanels({ orientation: "horizontal" });
inner.attachContainer(topSectionEl);     // <-- same el that's outer's panel 0
inner.attachPanel(sidebarEl,   0, { collapsible: true });
inner.attachPanel(editorEl,    1);
inner.attachPanel(inspectorEl, 2, { collapsible: true });
inner.attachHandle(innerHandle0El, 0);
inner.attachHandle(innerHandle1El, 1);
```

## API

```
createSplitPanels({
    orientation:    "horizontal" | "vertical",  // default horizontal
    layout?:        Signal<number[]>,           // controlled mode
    defaultLayout?: number[],                   // seed values (percentages)
    snapThreshold?: number,                     // collapse trigger, default 0.5
    keyboardStep?:  number,                     // %/keystep, default 5
    onLayoutChange?: (sizes, reason) => void,
})

  attachContainer(el)
  attachPanel(el, idx, { minSize?, maxSize?, defaultSize?, collapsible? })
  attachHandle(el, idx)         // handle between panel[idx] and panel[idx+1]

  layout()                      // current percentages
  setLayout(sizes, reason?)
  collapsePanel(idx)
  expandPanel(idx, sizeOverride?)
  destroy()
```

## CSS contract

The container gets `--lh-panel-0-pct`, `--lh-panel-1-pct`, ... as numeric
values (not unit-suffixed). Use them however you want:

```css
/* horizontal arrangement (panels side by side) */
.split[data-orientation="horizontal"] {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns:
        calc(var(--lh-panel-0-pct) * 1%)
        4px
        calc(var(--lh-panel-1-pct) * 1%);
}

/* vertical arrangement (panels stacked) */
.split[data-orientation="vertical"] {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-rows:
        calc(var(--lh-panel-0-pct) * 1%)
        4px
        calc(var(--lh-panel-1-pct) * 1%);
}
```

The container also gets `data-orientation="horizontal|vertical"` and
`data-resizing` (during an active drag). Each handle gets `data-dragging`.

## ARIA

Handles are auto-wired with `role="separator"`, `aria-orientation`,
`aria-valuenow`, `aria-valuemin`, `aria-valuemax`, `aria-valuetext`,
`tabindex="0"`.

### aria-orientation per the W3C spec

Per [WAI-ARIA 1.2 § separator](https://www.w3.org/TR/wai-aria-1.2/#separator)
and the [WAI-ARIA APG window-splitter pattern](https://www.w3.org/WAI/ARIA/apg/patterns/windowsplitter/),
`aria-orientation` describes **the separator line itself**, which is
**perpendicular** to the panel arrangement axis:

| `orientation` prop | panel arrangement   | separator visual | `aria-orientation` |
| ------------------ | ------------------- | ---------------- | ------------------ |
| `"horizontal"`     | side by side        | vertical bar     | `"vertical"`       |
| `"vertical"`       | stacked top/bottom  | horizontal bar   | `"horizontal"`     |

The primitive auto-flips the string so consumers don't have to think
about it. (This was previously inverted -- fixed in v0.7.16.)

### Keyboard

Each focused handle responds to:

- ArrowLeft / ArrowRight (horizontal orientation only) -- ±`keyboardStep`%
- ArrowUp / ArrowDown (vertical orientation only) -- ±`keyboardStep`%
- Home -- collapse the left/upper panel to its min
- End -- expand the left/upper panel to its max

## Custom element wrapper

```html
<lite-split-panels orientation="horizontal">
    <div data-panel data-min-size="15" data-default-size="25" data-collapsible>Side</div>
    <div data-handle></div>
    <div data-panel data-min-size="40">Main</div>
</lite-split-panels>
```

Dispatches `CustomEvent('layoutchange', { detail: { layout, reason } })`.

For nested layouts, just nest the elements -- each `<lite-split-panels>`
is an independent primitive instance with its own orientation and
layout.
